# Hypotheses by signal and metric

Use this file when diagnosing a weak metric or choosing the metric a new video should target.

| ID | Area / form | Hypothesis | Primary tactic | Best metric | First test |
|---:|---|---|---|---|---|
| H274 | Classification | If platform and viewer cannot classify the topic in 2 seconds, all downstream signals weaken. | algorithmic topic sentence | search/topic relevance | aligned speech/text/caption vs vague packaging |
| H275 | Classification | Repeating one exact problem phrase across speech, overlay and caption improves topic coherence. | semantic alignment | search traffic | one phrase aligned vs varied vague wording |
| H276 | Classification | Mixed-topic videos underperform single-promise videos when the audience is cold. | one promise only | avg watch, follows | single promise vs multi-topic |
| H277 | Viewed vs swiped | First-frame clarity has a larger effect on viewed-vs-swiped than final CTA choice. | first-frame lab | chose-to-view | five first frames, same body |
| H278 | Viewed vs swiped | Motion in the first half-second improves stop-scroll only when the subject is clear. | motion + clarity | 1-sec hold | clear motion vs ambiguous motion |
| H279 | Viewed vs swiped | A visible result beats a promise-only first frame for practical content. | show outcome | chose-to-view | result frame vs text-only claim |
| H280 | 1-second hold | A one-subject frame improves 1-second hold more than added text effects. | visual simplification | 1-sec hold | cluttered vs simplified frame |
| H281 | 1-second hold | Large faces help 1-second hold for unknown creators, but proof must follow quickly. | face → proof | 1-sec hold + saves | face-only vs face-to-proof |
| H282 | 1-second hold | Ambiguous opens can still work if the motion creates immediate curiosity without confusion. | controlled ambiguity | 1-sec hold + completion | clear open vs ambiguous intriguing open |
| H283 | 3-second retention | Problem-first hooks outperform topic-first hooks for cold educational content. | problem hook | 3-sec hold | topic hook vs problem hook |
| H284 | 3-second retention | Direct viewer callouts improve 3-second retention when the target audience is specific. | identity callout | 3-sec hold | generic vs 'if you post Shorts...' |
| H285 | 3-second retention | Hooks longer than one breath increase early drop-off unless paired with a strong visual payoff. | hook compression | 3-sec retention | short hook vs long hook |
| H286 | Average watch time | Average watch rises when each visual reset carries new information. | semantic resets | avg watch | decorative cuts vs proof cuts |
| H287 | Average watch time | Mid-video retention depends on micro-payoffs every 5–8 seconds for tutorial content. | micro-payoff rhythm | avg watch | single payoff vs staged payoffs |
| H288 | Average watch time | Voiceover pacing should slow at inspection moments and speed through setup. | variable pacing | avg watch | constant pace vs variable pace |
| H289 | Completion | Completion improves when the ending is foreshadowed but not fully resolved in the hook. | open loop with honest payoff | completion | direct answer vs foreshadowed reveal |
| H290 | Completion | Navigation text increases completion for videos longer than 45 seconds. | sections/chapters | completion | no sections vs sections |
| H291 | Completion | Shorter videos only improve completion if they preserve the first payoff and final resolution. | compression audit | completion | 60s full vs 30s compressed vs 15s overcut |
| H292 | Rewatch | Rewatch increases when the video contains a fast side-by-side comparison viewers want to inspect. | inspectable comparison | rewatch | sequential vs side-by-side |
| H293 | Rewatch | Looped endings increase rewatch but can reduce satisfaction if the loop hides the payoff. | honest loop | rewatch + sentiment | normal end vs seamless loop |
| H294 | Rewatch | Dense templates increase rewatch if the final frame summarises the formula. | template recap | rewatch, saves | no recap vs final formula |
| H295 | Saves | Saves increase when the video offers a reusable asset rather than a one-time insight. | checklist/template | save rate | tip vs checklist |
| H296 | Saves | Final screenshotable cards raise saves for frameworks and recipes. | screenshotable recap | save rate | spoken recap vs visual card |
| H297 | Saves | Save CTAs work only when the reason to save is explicit. | reasoned CTA | save rate | 'save this' vs 'save this before editing' |
| H298 | Sends / shares | Send CTAs beat generic share CTAs when they name a specific recipient. | recipient-specific CTA | send rate | share this vs send to X |
| H299 | Sends / shares | Identity-recognition videos send better than purely informational videos in community niches. | identity framing | send rate | tutorial vs identity-tutorial hybrid |
| H300 | Sends / shares | A warning frame increases sends when the recipient can make the same mistake. | social warning | sends | neutral tip vs warning |
| H301 | Comments | Binary prompts produce more comments than open-ended prompts. | A/B voting | comments/reach | thoughts? vs A/B |
| H302 | Comments | Comment quality improves when the prompt asks for a useful variable, such as niche, hook, or example. | data-gathering prompt | comment quality | generic prompt vs specific prompt |
| H303 | Comments | Controversial hooks increase comments but should be judged against completion and follow rate. | controversy control | comments + follows | contrarian vs neutral |
| H304 | Follows / subscriptions | Series labels increase follows by making future value predictable. | series identity | follows per 1k views | standalone vs series-labelled |
| H305 | Follows / subscriptions | Proof-backed content converts more follows than unsupported tips in expert niches. | evidence-led delivery | follow rate | tip vs proof-tip |
| H306 | Follows / subscriptions | High saves with low follows indicate the video is useful but the account promise is unclear. | profile/series bridge | save-to-follow conversion | normal ending vs series promise ending |
| H307 | Profile clicks | Profile clicks rise when the video creates authority curiosity rather than just giving a complete one-off answer. | authority gap | profile clicks | complete tip vs tip + series teaser |
| H308 | Profile clicks | Clear account positioning in pinned/profile content converts viral visitors better. | profile architecture | follows/profile visit | random profile vs pillar profile |
| H309 | Profile clicks | Strong creator POV increases profile clicks more than neutral utility. | distinct POV | profile clicks | neutral guide vs strong thesis |
| H310 | Search long-tail | Search traffic grows when the exact query appears in title/caption and first spoken line. | query alignment | 7d/30d search views | query-aligned vs clever title |
| H311 | Search long-tail | Search videos decay slower than trend videos if they solve a persistent problem. | evergreen problem | 30d views | search topic vs trend topic |
| H312 | Search long-tail | Hybrid search/feed opens are strongest when they show a result visually and name the query textually. | hybrid packaging | 24h + 30d views | direct query vs visual result vs hybrid |
| H313 | Trust / negative feedback | Clickbait hooks increase starts but reduce completion and follow conversion when the payoff is weaker than the promise. | accurate high-tension hook | completion, follows | clickbait vs accurate tension |
| H314 | Trust / negative feedback | Source/proof overlays reduce negative comments in controversial educational content. | evidence display | sentiment | claim-only vs source/proof |
| H315 | Trust / negative feedback | AI-looking generic templates reduce trust unless paired with original proof, voice or judgment. | originality signal | follows, sentiment | template video vs template + original proof |
